demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse; normally independent

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My stomach feels like it is going to explode, and I am constantly leaking urine into my pad but I can't actually pee. (Call made with my spouse here beside me.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The leaking has been going on for a long time but in the last day or two it has changed and now there is terrible pain in my lower belly and I still can't pass water properly.

symptom_narrative.onset: the leaking has been there for years, but it changed over the last 2 days and the severe belly pain built up over today
symptom_narrative.duration: years of leaking; 48 hours of the new constant dribbling; several hours of escalating pain
symptom_narrative.character: constant small dribbles into the pad; I keep trying to go but only a few drops come out, and my lower belly feels swollen and like it's going to burst
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the pain is really bad — I can't sit still, I'm pacing around the room
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: low down in my belly, above the pubic bone, in the middle
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: I try to pass water about every 20 minutes but only drops come, and it feels like my bladder never empties
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps building up on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever, no shaking chills, and I haven't seen any blood in the urine; my legs feel normal, no numbness
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: for years I've leaked a bit when I cough or lift, and sometimes I get a sudden urge and leak before I reach the toilet — but never this constant dribble with this pain
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm usually completely independent, but right now I can't sit or rest because of the pain

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing urine leakage — some when I cough or strain, and some sudden-urge leaking

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: an over-the-counter antihistamine I started recently for my hay fever / seasonal allergies

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with spouse who is present and helped make the call; normally manages independently at home

objective_data_offered.value: visibly distressed and sweaty, pacing the room, holding the lower abdomen, unable to sit still
spouse says he keeps rushing to the toilet every 20 minutes but only a few drops come out, and the pads are constantly wet
several soaked incontinence pads in the bathroom bin
